title: Past questions — Did you...?
description: Learn to ask and answer past tense questions confidently using Did, What, Where, When, Who, Why, How.

content.explanation: Form yes/no past questions by putting Did before the subject + BASE verb.

examples.question: Did she go to the market?

content.explanation: Add a question word before 'did' to ask for specific information.
content.structure: Question word + did + subject + base verb?

questions.example: What did you eat for lunch?
How long did the journey take?

tasks.title: Who as subject — no Did needed

content.explanation: When 'Who' is the SUBJECT of the question, we don't use Did.

examples.example: Who did you call? (you = subject, who = object)

content.instruction: Repeat with natural question intonation — voice rises for yes/no, falls for wh- questions.

phrases.text: Did you enjoy the film last night?
Where did you go for your holiday?
How long did the journey take?
Why did she leave so early?
Who did you meet at the party?

content.question: Turn into a question: 'She bought a new phone.'
options: Did she bought a new phone?
Did she buy a new phone?
Was she buy a new phone?
Did she buys a new phone?
content.explanation: Did + she + BASE verb. buy (not bought). Did she buy? ✓

content.question: You want to know the reason someone didn't come to work. Which question do you ask?
content.explanation: Reason → Why. Why didn't you come? ✓

content.question: '___ did you go after the meeting?' — 'I went to a café.'
content.explanation: The answer is a PLACE (café) → use Where.

content.question: '___ did the film last?' — 'About two and a half hours.'
content.explanation: Duration (time) → How long. How long did it last?

content.sentence: ___ did you travel to Delhi? By train or by plane?
content.explanation: The answer is about method of travel → How.

tasks.title: Fill in Did or question word

content.sentence: ___ did you buy at the market? And ___ did it cost?
content.explanation: Thing bought → What. Price → How much.

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a correct past Wh- question.
content.explanation: Why + didn't + subject + base verb? (negative question)

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a question, then its answer.
content.correct_sentence: How long did the journey take?
content.explanation: How long + did + subject + base verb?

content.instruction: Match each question to the most logical answer.

pairs.prompt: Where did you go last night?
pairs.answer: I went to a friend's house.

pairs.prompt: What did you eat for dinner?
pairs.answer: I had dal rice and salad.

pairs.prompt: Why didn't you come to class?
How long did the film last?
Who did you meet at the party?
pairs.answer: I met some old college friends.

content.instruction: Which question is grammatically correct?
content.wrong_sentence: What did you ate for breakfast?
options: What did you ate for breakfast?
What did you eat for breakfast?
What did ate you for breakfast?
content.explanation: Did + subject + BASE verb. eat (not ate). What did you eat? ✓

content.context: Meera just returned from a trip to Kerala. Her friend Raj asks her about it.

dialogue.speaker: Raj
dialogue.line: Welcome back! How was Kerala? Did you enjoy it?

dialogue.speaker: Meera
dialogue.line: It was absolutely wonderful! I loved every minute.

dialogue.speaker: Raj
dialogue.line: Where exactly did you go?

dialogue.speaker: Meera
dialogue.line: We went to Alleppey for the backwaters and then to Munnar for the tea gardens.

dialogue.speaker: Raj
dialogue.line: How long did the trip take?

dialogue.speaker: Meera
dialogue.line: Five days. We left on Wednesday and came back on Sunday.

dialogue.speaker: Raj
dialogue.line: Did you take a houseboat?

dialogue.speaker: Meera
dialogue.line: Yes! We stayed on a houseboat for one night — it was the most amazing experience!

dialogue.speaker: Raj
dialogue.line: Who did you go with?

dialogue.speaker: Meera
dialogue.line: My sister and two friends. Four of us altogether.

dialogue.speaker: Raj
dialogue.line: What did you eat there?

dialogue.speaker: Meera
dialogue.line: So much fish! And appam with stew. It was delicious. I didn't want to leave!

comprehension_questions.question: Where did Meera go on her trip?
How many people went on the trip?
What was the most amazing experience?

content.scenario: You want to know why your colleague came to work very late this morning.
content.question: Which is the most natural and polite question?
options: Why did you came late today?
Why did you come in late today?
content.explanation: Why + did + you + BASE verb. come (not came). Why did you come in late? ✓

content.instruction: Play both roles. Ask 6 different past questions about a holiday and give detailed answers.
content.prompt: Interview your friend about their last holiday. Ask: Where, When, How long, Who with, What did you do, Did you enjoy it, What did you eat.
content.example_response: Where did you go for your last holiday? — I went to Coorg in Karnataka. When did you go? — We went in March last year. How long did you stay? — About four days. Who did you go with? — I went with my family — four of us. What did you do there? — We visited a coffee plantation, went on nature walks, and ate at local restaurants. Did you enjoy it? — Absolutely! It was one of the best holidays I ever had. What was the food like? — Amazing — fresh, simple, and delicious.
key_phrases: Where/When/Who/What/How long/Did + did + subject + base verb?

content.instruction: Answer 5 past tense questions about yourself. Give full, detailed answers.
content.prompt: Answer: 1) What did you do last Saturday? 2) Where did you go last month? 3) Who did you talk to yesterday? 4) What did you eat for your last birthday dinner? 5) How did you feel when you finished school or college?
content.example_response: Last Saturday I went to a family gathering — we had a small celebration for my uncle's birthday. Last month I went to Pondicherry for a day trip with friends. Yesterday I talked to my manager about a project, and also called my mother in the evening. For my last birthday dinner I ate at a rooftop restaurant — I had grilled chicken and a wonderful chocolate cake. When I finished college I felt absolutely proud and a little scared — I didn't know what the future held!
key_phrases: I went / ate / talked / felt / finished / had / called + details

content.sentence: A: ___ did you do last weekend? B: I ___ to my friend's house. A: ___ did you go? B: On Saturday evening. A: ___ did you get home? B: I ___ home quite late — around midnight.
content.explanation: What (thing/activity), went (go→went), When (time), What time (specific time), came (come→came).

what_you_learned: Yes/No: Did + subject + BASE verb? → Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.
What (thing), Where (place), When (time), Who (person), Why (reason), How (manner), How long (duration), How much (price)
All wh- questions: Question word + did + subject + BASE verb?
Never use irregular/past form in questions: Did she go ✓, Did she went ✗
Who as subject: Who came? (no did needed)
content.next_lesson_title: My last holiday — telling a story

title: My last holiday — telling a story
description: Learn to tell a complete past story using narrative connectors, all past tenses, and descriptive language.

content.explanation: Use these words to connect events in a story and make it flow naturally.

connectors.example: First, we took the train to Ooty.
Then we checked into our hotel.
Next, we visited the tea gardens.
Later in the afternoon, we went shopping.
Finally, we took a long train ride home.

content.explanation: A good story uses all three past forms naturally.

usage.use: State, description, feeling at that time

content.example_story_sentence: The day was sunny (was), so we went to the beach (irregular), ate fresh fish (irregular), and walked for hours (regular). It was the best day of the trip (was)!

content.explanation: Useful words for talking about holidays and travel experiences.

items.note: book a hotel, reserve a table
visiting famous places as a tourist
a gift you buy to remember a place
extremely beautiful — the view was breathtaking

content.instruction: Repeat naturally with good rhythm. Storytelling has a flowing pace.

phrases.text: First, we took the train to Mysore. Then we checked into our hotel.
The view from the top was absolutely breathtaking!
Suddenly, it started to rain heavily and we ran inside.
Luckily, we found a great restaurant nearby.
Finally, we came home tired but very happy.

content.question: You want to describe the LAST event in your story. Which word do you use?
content.explanation: Finally / In the end = the last event in a sequence.

content.question: Something unexpected and bad happened. Which word do you use?
content.explanation: Unfortunately = bad unexpected event. Luckily = good unexpected event.

content.question: Choose the sentence that uses ALL past forms correctly:
options: The weather was beautiful so we go to the beach and swam.
The weather was beautiful so we went to the beach and swam.
The weather is beautiful so we went to the beach and swam.
The weather was beautiful so we went to the beach and swimmed.
content.explanation: was (state) ✓, went (go→went) ✓, swam (swim→swam) ✓ — all correct past forms.

content.sentence: ___, we checked into the hotel. ___, we went for a walk. ___, it started to rain. ___, a nearby café was open so we went inside.
content.explanation: Story order: First→Then→Suddenly (unexpected bad)→Luckily (unexpected good).

tasks.title: Fill in all past forms in a story

content.sentence: Last month I ___ (go) to Hampi with my friends. The ruins ___ (be) absolutely breathtaking. We ___ (walk) for hours and ___ (take) many photos. Then we ___ (eat) at a local restaurant — the food ___ (be) delicious!
content.explanation: went, were, walked, took, ate, was — mix of irregular, regular, and was/were.

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a correct past narrative sentence.
content.correct_sentence: The view from the top was breathtaking.
content.explanation: The + noun + from + the top + was + adjective.

content.instruction: Match each narrative connector to when you use it.

pairs.answer: the last event in the story

content.context: Vikram tells his colleague Ananya about his trip to Rajasthan.

dialogue.speaker: Ananya
dialogue.line: I heard you went to Rajasthan! How was it?

dialogue.speaker: Vikram
dialogue.line: It was incredible! We went to Jaipur first. The Pink City was absolutely breathtaking.

dialogue.speaker: Ananya
dialogue.line: What did you do there?

dialogue.speaker: Vikram
dialogue.line: First, we visited Amber Fort — it was magnificent. Then we went to the bazaars and bought beautiful textiles and jewellery.

dialogue.speaker: Ananya
dialogue.line: Did anything unexpected happen?

dialogue.speaker: Vikram
dialogue.line: Yes! Suddenly, our auto broke down on the way to the hotel. Unfortunately, we had to walk in the heat for twenty minutes!

dialogue.speaker: Ananya
dialogue.line: Oh no! Was everything else okay?

dialogue.speaker: Vikram
dialogue.line: Luckily, yes! After that, everything was perfect. We went to Jodhpur next — the Blue City. It was stunning.

dialogue.speaker: Ananya
dialogue.line: What was the food like?

dialogue.speaker: Vikram
dialogue.line: Amazing! I ate dal baati churma for the first time and it was the best food I tasted on the trip.

dialogue.speaker: Ananya
dialogue.line: And how did the trip end?

dialogue.speaker: Vikram
dialogue.line: Finally, we came back to Chennai on a night train. I was exhausted but I already want to go back!

comprehension_questions.question: What went wrong on the trip?
How did they travel back to Chennai?

content.instruction: Choose the correctly written version of this holiday story.
content.wrong_text: Last year I goed to Goa with my friends. First we checked in our hotel. Then we go to the beach and swimed. The water was clear and the view was breathtaken.
options: Last year I went to Goa with my friends. First we checked into our hotel. Then we went to the beach and swam. The water was clear and the view was breathtaking.
Last year I went to Goa with my friends. First we checked in our hotel. Then we go to the beach and swim. The water was clear and the view was breathtaking.
Last year I went to Goa with my friends. First we checked into our hotel. Then we went to the beach and swimed. The water was clear and view was breathtaking.
Last year I goed to Goa with my friends. First we checked into hotel. Then we went to beach and swam. The water clear and view was breathtaking.
content.explanation: went ✓ (not goed), checked into ✓, went ✓ (not go), swam ✓ (not swimed), breathtaking ✓ (adjective).

content.scenario: You are telling a story. Something very unexpected and bad happened in the middle — you got lost!
content.question: Which sentence most naturally introduces this event?
options: Suddenly, we realised we were completely lost!
content.explanation: Suddenly = unexpected event. Suddenly, we realised... adds drama and surprise. ✓

content.instruction: Speak for 75 seconds. Tell a complete story about a holiday, trip, or day out you had. Use narrative connectors, all past forms, and descriptive adjectives.
content.prompt: Tell me about the best (or most interesting!) trip or day out you remember. Use First, Then, After that, Suddenly, Luckily/Unfortunately, Finally.
content.example_response: Last year I went on an amazing trip to Munnar in Kerala with my family. First, we took an overnight bus from Chennai and arrived early in the morning. The mountains were covered in mist — it was absolutely stunning. Then we checked into our homestay — the hosts were incredibly warm and welcoming. After that, we visited a tea plantation. I walked through the green fields and drank the freshest tea I ever tasted. Suddenly, a huge elephant appeared on the road nearby! We were a bit scared but also very excited. Luckily, it walked away peacefully. In the evening we ate a traditional Kerala meal — it was delicious. Unfortunately, the trip was only three days. Finally, we came home on Sunday feeling relaxed and very happy. I want to go back very soon!
key_phrases: First / Then / After that / Suddenly / Luckily / Unfortunately / Finally

content.instruction: Ask 6 questions about a friend's recent trip. Then give imaginary answers as your friend.
content.prompt: Your friend just came back from a trip. Interview them about it.
content.example_response: Where did you go? — I went to Pondicherry. How long did you stay? — Three days. Who did you go with? — I went with my sister. What did you do there? — We visited the French Quarter, ate amazing seafood, and walked along the promenade. Did anything unexpected happen? — Yes! Suddenly it rained heavily on our second day but luckily we found a beautiful café and spent three hours there. What did you enjoy most? — The food was breathtaking — we ate at a little restaurant right by the sea.

tasks.title: Speaking — A day that went wrong

content.instruction: Speak for 60 seconds. Tell a story about a day when things didn't go according to plan. Use Unfortunately, Suddenly, Luckily.
content.prompt: Tell me about a day when everything went wrong (or almost wrong!).
content.example_response: Last month I had a really difficult day. First, I woke up late because my alarm didn't ring. Then I rushed to get ready but I couldn't find my keys — I looked everywhere! Unfortunately, I missed my bus and had to take an auto. Then suddenly the auto broke down in traffic and I was stuck for twenty minutes! I was very stressed. Luckily, my colleague covered for me in the meeting. After work I wanted to go to the gym but I didn't go because I was completely exhausted. Finally, I came home, ate a simple dinner, and went to bed at 9pm. It wasn't my best day!

content.sentence: Last summer ___ (be) the best of my life. I ___ (travel) to Kerala with my family. First we ___ (go) to Alleppey and ___ (take) a houseboat. The backwaters ___ (be) beautiful and peaceful. Then we ___ (drive) to Munnar. Unfortunately the weather ___ (be) a bit cloudy but we still ___ (see) stunning views. Finally we ___ (come) home feeling completely refreshed.
content.explanation: was(summer), travelled(regular), went, took, were(backwaters), drove(drive→drove), was(weather), saw, came.

content.instruction: Match each holiday vocabulary word to its definition.

pairs.answer: a gift bought to remember a place
so beautiful it takes your breath away
arrive and register at a hotel
organised visit with someone showing the way

what_you_learned: Narrative connectors: First, Then, After that, Later, Next, Finally, Suddenly, Unfortunately, Luckily
Story uses all 3 past forms together: was/were + regular -ed + irregular
Holiday vocabulary: check in, sightseeing, souvenir, breathtaking, guided tour
Storytelling tip: use adjectives and connectors to make stories interesting
content.next_lesson_title: Childhood memories — when I was young

title: Childhood memories — when I was young
description: Talk about childhood using was/were, past verbs, used to (introduction), and memory vocabulary.

tasks.title: When I was young / a child

content.explanation: Use these phrases to set the context for childhood memories.

phrases.phrase: When I was young / a child / little
phrases.example: When I was young, I loved climbing trees.
When I was ten, I got my first bicycle.

phrases.phrase: As a child / As a kid
phrases.example: As a child, I was very shy.
In my childhood, we didn't have mobile phones.
I grew up in a small village.

content.explanation: 'Used to' describes habits or states that were TRUE in the past but are NOT true now.

examples.sentence: I used to wake up very early.
examples.note: I woke up early as a child — but not now

examples.sentence: We used to play outside all day.
She used to have long hair.
examples.note: She had long hair — she cut it

examples.sentence: I didn't use to like vegetables.
Did you use to walk to school?
examples.note: Question: Did + subject + use to + base?

content.vs_simple_past: Simple past: happened once. Used to: happened regularly/continuously in the past.

content.explanation: Vocabulary to talk about growing up and childhood memories.

items.note: Where you studied as a child
Friend from when you were young

content.instruction: Repeat with a warm, nostalgic tone — childhood memories are happy!

phrases.text: When I was young, I used to climb trees with my friends.
I grew up in a small village near Chennai.
I didn't use to like school — I preferred playing outside!
My best friend and I used to walk to school together every day.
As a child I was quite naughty — my teachers knew my name very well!

content.question: 'I ___ love sweets when I was a child, but now I prefer savoury food.'
content.explanation: Positive form: used to + base verb. I used to love. ✓

content.question: 'She ___ like reading but now she loves it.'
content.explanation: Negative: didn't use to + base verb. didn't use to like ✓

content.question: Which sentence describes a REPEATED habit in the past (not just once)?
options: I used to walk to school every day.
I walked to school on Monday.
I was at school last week.
content.explanation: 'Used to' = repeated habit in the past. 'Every day' confirms it was regular.

content.sentence: As a child I ___ (play) cricket every evening after school. We ___ (not have) video games so we ___ (spend) all our time outdoors.
content.explanation: All habits/states as a child → used to. Negative = didn't use to.

tasks.title: Mix: was/were + used to + simple past

content.sentence: When I ___ (be) eight, I ___ (be) very shy. I ___ (not use to) speak in class. Then one day my teacher ___ (ask) me to tell a story and I ___ (love) it!
content.explanation: was (age/state), was (shy=state), didn't use to (habit), asked (one time), loved (one-time reaction).

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a correct childhood memory sentence.
content.correct_sentence: I used to play outside with my friends every day.
content.explanation: Subject + used to + base verb + place + with + person + time.

content.instruction: Match each description to the correct childhood vocabulary word.

pairs.prompt: Long break from school in hot season
The town or city where you grew up
Friend from when you were very young

content.context: Two colleagues Amrit and Shalini share childhood memories during a lunch break.

dialogue.speaker: Amrit
dialogue.line: Shalini, where did you grow up?

dialogue.speaker: Shalini
dialogue.line: I grew up in a small town in Tamil Nadu — Kumbakonam. And you?

dialogue.speaker: Amrit
dialogue.line: I grew up in Chennai. Were you happy as a child?

dialogue.speaker: Shalini
dialogue.line: Very! We used to play outside all evening. There were so many kids in the street.

dialogue.speaker: Amrit
dialogue.line: Did you use to walk to school?

dialogue.speaker: Shalini
dialogue.line: Yes, every day! My best friend and I used to walk together and talk the whole way.

dialogue.speaker: Amrit
dialogue.line: What were you like at school? Were you a good student?

dialogue.speaker: Shalini
dialogue.line: I was quite studious actually! But I was also a bit naughty sometimes. My maths teacher used to call me by my full name when I was in trouble!

dialogue.speaker: Amrit
dialogue.line: Ha! I didn't use to like school much. I used to pretend I was sick to stay home!

dialogue.speaker: Shalini
dialogue.line: Really? What did you enjoy doing as a kid?

dialogue.speaker: Amrit
dialogue.line: I used to read comics, play cricket, and watch cartoons all day. Simple times!

comprehension_questions.question: What did Shalini and her best friend do every day?
How did Amrit feel about school?
options: He didn't use to like it much

content.instruction: Which sentence uses 'used to' correctly?
content.wrong_sentence: When I was young, I use to wake up early and I used to played outside.
options: When I was young, I use to wake up early and I used to played outside.
When I was young, I used to wake up early and I used to play outside.
When I was young, I used to woke up early and I used to play outside.
When I was young, I used to wake up early and I use to play outside.
content.explanation: used to + BASE verb. wake (not woke), play (not played). I used to wake... used to play ✓

content.scenario: Someone asks: 'Are you a morning person?' You weren't as a child but now you are.
content.question: Which is the most natural reply?
options: Yes I am. I am morning person.
Yes! I used to sleep late as a child but now I wake up early naturally.
Yes. I didn't sleep early before but now yes.
Yes! I use to sleep late but I wake up early.
content.explanation: used to sleep late (past habit, not now) + now I wake up early (present change). Natural contrast. ✓

content.instruction: Speak for 70 seconds. Describe your childhood — where you grew up, what you used to do, what you were like. Include used to at least 5 times.
content.prompt: Tell me about your childhood. What do you remember most clearly? What did you use to do?
content.example_response: I grew up in a small town called Vellore in Tamil Nadu. As a child I was quite active and a bit naughty! I used to wake up very early and go outside to play before school. My friends and I used to play cricket and tag in the evenings until it was dark. When I was young I didn't use to like studying much — I preferred playing! But I used to love reading comics and adventure stories. In summer holidays we used to visit my grandparents in a village. Those were wonderful times — we used to help with the cows, eat fresh mangoes from the tree, and sleep on the terrace under the stars. I didn't use to appreciate it then but now I miss it deeply.
key_phrases: As a child / When I was young

content.instruction: Speak for 50 seconds. Compare your life as a child to your life now. Use 'used to' for the past and present tense for now.
content.prompt: How is your life different now from when you were a child?
content.example_response: My life is very different now! When I was a child I used to sleep ten hours a night — now I sleep only six. I used to eat everything my mother cooked without complaint. Now I cook for myself and I'm much more careful about what I eat. I didn't use to care about money — now I think about it every day! I used to have so much free time — now I have almost none. I used to laugh at everything. I still laugh but I also worry more. I miss the simplicity of childhood sometimes!
key_phrases: I used to ___ but now I ___
I didn't use to ___ but now I ___

content.sentence: When I ___ (be) a child, I ___ (grow) up in a village. I ___ (use to / walk) to school with my older sister every morning. We ___ (not / use to / take) the bus — it ___ (be) too expensive. Our school ___ (be) small but our teacher ___ (be) wonderful. She ___ (use to / tell) us stories every Friday and we all ___ (love) it.
content.explanation: was (age), grew (grow→grew), used to walk (habit), didn't use to take (negative habit), was×3 (states/descriptions), used to tell (teacher's habit), loved (simple past reaction).

content.instruction: Speak for 60 seconds. Describe one specific favourite memory from your childhood in detail. Use was/were, simple past, and used to.
content.prompt: What is your favourite memory from childhood? Describe it as if you are re-living it.
content.example_response: My favourite memory is from summer holidays when I was about nine years old. We used to go to my grandmother's house in the village every May. The house was big and old with a huge mango tree in the garden. Every morning we used to wake up early and pick fresh mangoes — they were sweet and absolutely delicious. In the evenings all the cousins used to play games on the terrace. My grandmother used to tell us stories at night before we slept under the stars. I remember feeling completely safe and happy. Those evenings were magical. I didn't use to realise how special they were at the time, but now I treasure that memory deeply.

content.instruction: This childhood story has 4 errors. Choose the correct version.
content.wrong_text: When I was young I use to love football. I played every day after school. My friends and I used to went to the park near our house. We didn't used to come home until it was dark. Those were the best days of my life.
options: When I was young I used to love football. I played every day after school. My friends and I used to go to the park near our house. We didn't use to come home until it was dark. Those were the best days of my life.

When I was young I used to love football. I played every day after school. My friends and I used to went to the park near our house. We didn't use to come home until it was dark. Those were the best days of my life.
When I was young I use to love football. I played every day after school. My friends and I used to go to the park. We didn't use to come home until dark. Those were the best days of my life.

When I was young I used to love football. I played every day after school. My friends and I used to go to the park near our house. We didn't used to come home until it was dark. Those are the best days of my life.
content.explanation: use to→used to (positive), used to went→used to go (base verb), didn't used to→didn't use to. Those were (past) ✓.

what_you_learned: Context phrases: When I was young, As a child, In my childhood, I grew up in
Used to + base verb = past habit no longer true (I used to wake up early.)
Negative: didn't use to + base verb (I didn't use to like vegetables.)
Question: Did you use to ___? (Did you use to walk to school?)
Childhood vocabulary: naughty, well-behaved, strict, kind, summer holidays, hometown
Three past forms together: was/were (state) + used to (habit) + simple past (specific event)
content.next_lesson_title: A1 Unit 4 Review + Final Exit Test

title: Unit 4 Review — Talking about the past
description: Final review of all Unit 4 past tenses: was/were, regular -ed, irregular verbs, didn't, did questions, and used to.

content.explanation: Everything you learned in Unit 4 — the complete A1 past tense system.

examples: I used to walk to school.

content.question: 'My parents ___ very happy when I got the job.'
content.explanation: My parents = They (plural) → were.

content.question: Choose the correct past form of 'carry'.
content.explanation: Consonant + y → change y to i + ed. carry → carried ✓

content.question: 'Last night she ___ a long email to her manager.'
content.explanation: Write is irregular → wrote. She wrote a long email. ✓

content.question: 'He ___ to the meeting because he forgot about it.'
content.explanation: didn't + BASE verb. come (not came). He didn't come. ✓

content.question: '___ you ___ breakfast this morning?'
content.explanation: Did + subject + BASE verb. eat (not ate). Did you eat? ✓

content.question: Which sentence correctly describes a past habit?
options: I use to go to school by bus.
I used to went to school by bus.
I used to go to school by bus.
I was use to go to school by bus.
content.explanation: used to + BASE verb. go (not went). I used to go. ✓

content.question: Which is the BEST sentence to describe a habit that was true for many years in the past?
options: I used to walk to school every day for ten years.
content.explanation: Long repeated past habit → used to. I used to walk (every day for ten years). ✓

content.sentence: Last week I ___ (go) to a wedding. It ___ (be) beautiful. I ___ (wear) a new outfit and ___ (meet) many old friends. I ___ (not know) half the people but everyone ___ (be) very friendly.
content.explanation: went ✓, was ✓, wore (wear→wore) ✓, met ✓, didn't know ✓ (base after didn't), were (everyone treated as plural here) ✓.

content.sentence: I ___ (used to / wake up) late every morning but now I wake up at 6am. I ___ (used to / not enjoy) exercise but now I go to the gym every day. My whole lifestyle ___ (change) completely!
content.explanation: used to wake up ✓, didn't use to enjoy ✓ (negative), changed ✓ (regular past).

tasks.title: Review — Build the past story sentence

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a correct, complete past sentence with a connector.
content.correct_sentence: We arrived and then checked into the hotel. We were very tired.
content.explanation: arrived (regular) + then + checked (regular) + were very tired (state).

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a correct past question.
content.correct_sentence: How long did the journey take?
content.explanation: How long + did + subject + base verb?

content.instruction: This paragraph has 4 past tense errors. Which version corrects all of them?
content.wrong_text: Last year I use to go to the gym every day. Then I injuried my knee and I didn't went for three months. When I came back I was surprise — I felt so weak! Fortunately I recovered quickly and now I exercise regular.
options: Last year I used to go to the gym every day. Then I injured my knee and I didn't go for three months. When I came back I was surprised — I felt so weak! Fortunately I recovered quickly and now I exercise regularly.

Last year I used to go to the gym every day. Then I injured my knee and I didn't went for three months. When I came back I was surprised — I felt so weak! Fortunately I recovered quickly and now I exercise regularly.

Last year I use to go to the gym every day. Then I injured my knee and I didn't go for three months. When I came back I was surprised — I felt so weak! Fortunately I recovered quickly and now I exercise regularly.

Last year I used to go to the gym every day. Then I injured my knee and I didn't go for three months. When I came back I was surprise — I felt so weak! Fortunately I recovered quickly and now I exercise regular.
content.explanation: use to→used to, injuried→injured (regular), didn't went→didn't go (base), surprise→surprised (adjective), regular→regularly (adverb).

tasks.title: Review — Match past form to rule

content.instruction: Match each sentence to the grammar rule it demonstrates.

pairs.prompt: She cooked dinner and cleaned up.
I used to play cricket as a child.

content.context: At a school reunion, two old friends Kavitha and Suresh see each other after 15 years.

dialogue.speaker: Kavitha
dialogue.line: Suresh! Is that you? You haven't changed at all!

dialogue.speaker: Suresh
dialogue.line: Kavitha! Wow, fifteen years! How are you?

dialogue.speaker: Kavitha
dialogue.line: I'm great, thank you! Do you remember our old classroom?

dialogue.speaker: Suresh
dialogue.line: Of course! We used to sit in the front row together. I was terrified of Mr Sharma!

dialogue.speaker: Kavitha
dialogue.line: He was very strict! But do you remember the school trips we took?

dialogue.speaker: Suresh
dialogue.line: Yes! We went to Mysore in Class 8. It was incredible — we visited the palace and ate at a big hotel.

dialogue.speaker: Kavitha
dialogue.line: I remember you were so naughty on that trip! Didn't you get into trouble?

dialogue.speaker: Suresh
dialogue.line: I didn't mean to! I accidentally set off the fire alarm in the hotel. The teacher was absolutely furious!

dialogue.speaker: Kavitha
dialogue.line: I forgot about that! What did she say to you?

dialogue.speaker: Suresh
dialogue.line: She didn't speak to me for two days! But then we laughed about it later.

dialogue.speaker: Kavitha
dialogue.line: Those were amazing days. I used to think school was boring — now I miss it so much!

dialogue.speaker: Suresh
dialogue.line: Me too. We were young and carefree. Life was simpler then.

comprehension_questions.question: Where did the class trip go in Class 8?
What did Suresh accidentally do at the hotel?
How did Kavitha feel about school as a child?

tasks.title: A1 Final speaking test — Part 1: Last weekend

content.instruction: Speak for 70 seconds. Describe your last weekend using ALL past forms: was/were, regular -ed, irregular past, and didn't.
content.prompt: Tell me everything about your last weekend from Saturday morning to Sunday night.
content.example_response: Last weekend was really enjoyable. On Saturday morning I woke up quite late — about 9am. Then I cooked breakfast and cleaned the house. In the afternoon I went to the market and bought vegetables and fruit. It was quite crowded! Then I met a friend for coffee. We sat in a nice café and talked for about two hours. In the evening I cooked a proper meal — I made dal and rice. I didn't watch TV that night — I read a book instead and went to sleep early. On Sunday I didn't do very much — I was quite tired. I stayed home, did some laundry, called my parents, and rested. It wasn't an exciting weekend but it was peaceful and I felt very refreshed on Monday morning.

tasks.title: A1 Final speaking test — Part 2: Childhood

content.instruction: Speak for 65 seconds. Talk about your childhood using used to, was/were, and past verbs. Describe at least ONE specific memory.
content.prompt: Tell me about your childhood and one specific memory you treasure.
content.example_response: I grew up in a town called Tiruppur. As a child I was very active and a bit naughty! I used to play cricket every evening with my friends in the street. We didn't use to go home until it was dark. My mother used to call us from the balcony when dinner was ready. One memory I treasure is from when I was about eight years old. My father took me to watch my first cricket match at the stadium. We went by bus — it was very exciting. The crowd was enormous and noisy. I don't remember who won but I remember feeling the most excited I ever felt in my life. My father used to say it was one of our best days together. I still think about it sometimes.

tasks.title: A1 Final speaking test — Part 3: Ask past questions

content.instruction: Ask 5 past questions and give answers. Use What, Where, When, Why, How long, Did.
content.prompt: Imagine you are interviewing someone about a memorable event in their life. Ask 5 good questions and give detailed answers.
content.example_response: What was the most exciting thing you did last year? — I went trekking in Coorg with three friends. Where exactly did you go? — We hiked through the coffee estates near Madikeri — it was stunning. How long did the trip take? — Four days. Did you face any difficulties? — Yes! On the second day it rained heavily and we got a bit lost but luckily a local farmer showed us the way. Why did you choose Coorg? — Because I used to dream about going there as a child — I finally made it happen!
key_phrases: What/Where/When/Why/How long + did + subject + base verb?

content.sentence: When I ___ (be) a teenager, I ___ (use to / love) football. Every weekend my friends and I ___ (go) to the park and ___ (play) for hours. One day we ___ (see) a scout from a local team — he ___ (watch) us. He ___ (come) over and ___ (ask) if I wanted to join. I ___ (be) absolutely thrilled! I ___ (not tell) my parents immediately because I ___ (be) nervous. Finally I ___ (say) yes and it ___ (be) one of the best decisions I ever made.
content.explanation: Comprehensive test of all A1 past tense forms: was/were, used to, irregular, regular, didn't+base.

content.congratulations: OUTSTANDING! You have completed ALL FOUR UNITS of the A1 level. You can now speak about yourself, your family, your daily life, your health, your home, and your past experiences — all in correct English. You are ready for A2!
all_a1_grammar_mastered: Simple present: I work / She works + negatives and questions
Present continuous: I am working / She is working
Possessive adjectives: my, your, his, her, our, their
Simple past regular: worked, walked, studied
Simple past irregular: went, ate, saw, bought, felt, slept...
Past questions: Did + subject + base verb? + wh- questions
Adjectives + intensifiers: very, quite, a bit, absolutely
content.next_unit_title: Unit 5 — Plans & future: going to, will, invitations
